title: Usage-metrics telemetry opt-out
why_it_matters: Claude Code sends anonymous usage metrics (latency, reliability, and usage patterns) to Anthropic and third-party logging infrastructure by default for direct Claude API usage — off by default for every alternative model-provider integration documented in Anthropic's 'Default behaviors by API provider' table (as of this retrieval: Google Cloud's Agent Platform, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS, each gated behind its own opt-in environment variable; this table may grow further opt-in providers over time). Anthropic's documentation states metrics never include code, prompts, or file paths, and that setting the environment variable DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1 opts out. Anthropic's settings reference documents no dedicated boolean settings.json key for this control; DISABLE_TELEMETRY is documented only as an environment variable. The same settings reference states that 'All environment variables can be checked into settings.json' via its documented, user-scope env object, so this rule observes DISABLE_TELEMETRY only as embedded at that path (env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY) inside the inspected user-level settings.json. It cannot observe an ambient shell- or process-level DISABLE_TELEMETRY set outside this file, and it does not evaluate the separate DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING, DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY, or CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC controls — the first three now have their own dedicated rules (claude-code-error-reporting-opt-out-01, claude-code-feedback-command-opt-out-01, claude-code-feedback-survey-opt-out-01).

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level settings.json sets env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY = "1", opting out of Claude Code's usage-metrics telemetry.

outcomes.when: env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective usage-metrics telemetry posture cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers and the ambient process environment may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY is unset in the user-level config; usage metrics are on by default for direct Claude API usage but off by default for every alternative model-provider integration unless explicitly enabled, this rule does not determine which provider is active, and uninspected managed, project, local, or CLI-flag layers plus the ambient shell environment may also set DISABLE_TELEMETRY outside this file.

outcomes.when: env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1"
outcomes.message: env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1" — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY is set to a value other than the documented opt-out token "1" — raw values are never displayed

sources.title: Data usage
sources.notes: Under 'Telemetry services': 'Claude Code sends two kinds of operational telemetry: usage metrics and error reports... Metrics: latency, reliability, and usage patterns, sent to Anthropic and to third-party logging infrastructure over TLS. Metrics never include your code, prompts, or file paths. Set DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1 to opt out.' The 'Default behaviors by API provider' table documents metrics as default-on for direct Claude API usage and default-off for Google Cloud's Agent Platform, Amazon Bedrock, Microsoft Foundry, and Claude Platform on AWS, absent their own opt-in variables, and separately: 'All environment variables can be checked into settings.json (see settings reference).' Re-fetched and re-verified 2026-07-17; content hash unchanged from the prior retrieval — this note corrects an earlier version of this rule that enumerated only three of the four non-default providers.

sources.title: Claude Code settings
sources.notes: Documents the top-level env settings.json key: 'Environment variables applied to every session,' example {"FOO": "bar"}, and documents no dedicated boolean telemetry key. Also documents the user scope resolving to ~/.claude/settings.json (lowest precedence) — the only scope this rule inspects.

limitations: Only the user-level ~/.claude/settings.json env object is inspected; managed, project, local, and CLI-flag scopes are not inspected and can supply or override the effective value, and this rule cannot observe DISABLE_TELEMETRY set directly in the shell or process environment outside settings.json.

Default telemetry posture is provider-dependent (on by default for direct Anthropic API/Console usage; off by default for every alternative model-provider integration unless explicitly enabled); this rule does not determine which provider is active and never infers the effective default from account or environment state.

This rule observes only the usage-metrics opt-out; error reporting (DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING), the /feedback command (DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND), and session quality surveys (CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY) now have their own dedicated rules (claude-code-error-reporting-opt-out-01, claude-code-feedback-command-opt-out-01, claude-code-feedback-survey-opt-out-01); the broader CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC switch remains a documented control no rule here evaluates, since no explicit "=1" value is documented for it, only "is set"/"setting it".
Metrics content is described by Anthropic's documentation as excluding code, prompts, and file paths; this rule does not independently verify that claim — no local file can.

No settings.json key named env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY appears as a worked example in either retrieved page; this rule relies on the documented, general statement that any environment variable, including DISABLE_TELEMETRY, may be checked into settings.json's env object. Any value other than the documented "1" opt-out token has no confirmed documented effect and is treated as unrecognized rather than assumed to enable or disable telemetry.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers and the ambient process environment are not inspected.
env.DISABLE_TELEMETRY set to a value other than "1" (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
